# FineCheck

> Free UK parking fine appeal tool. Check if your fine is valid, understand your legal rights, and generate professional appeal letters. Covers private parking fines, council PCNs, bus lane fines, congestion charges, and ULEZ penalties. No signup required.

## What It Does

FineCheck helps UK drivers challenge unfair parking fines and penalties. Users describe their situation and receive:
- Instant fine validity assessment
- NtK (Notice to Keeper) timing analysis under POFA 2012
- Identification of the correct appeal body (POPLA, IAS, or Traffic Penalty Tribunal)
- Common grounds for appeal based on operator or council
- Appeal strength rating (strong, moderate, weak)
- Professional AI-generated appeal letters citing specific UK legislation and case law

## Pricing

| Plan | Price | What's Included |
|------|-------|----------------|
| Free Assessment | Free | Validity check, NtK timing analysis, appeal body ID, grounds analysis, strength rating, step-by-step guide |
| Standard Letter | £5.99 (one-time) | Everything free + AI-generated legal letter citing Acts of Parliament, verified case law, operator-specific arguments, professional PDF |
| Premium Pack | £9.99 (one-time) | Everything standard + escalation letter for POPLA/IAS/Tribunal, evidence checklist, operator weakness analysis, second-stage appeal |

No subscriptions. No hidden fees. Pay once per fine.

## Who It's For

- Drivers who received a private parking fine (ParkingEye, UKPC, Excel, APCOA, Smart Parking, NCP, Euro Car Parks, Indigo)
- Drivers who received a council PCN (Penalty Charge Notice)
- People fined at supermarkets, hospitals, retail parks, gyms, train stations, universities, airports, hotels, or schools
- Anyone who received a bus lane, congestion charge, ULEZ, or red route fine
- Drivers who were not driving when the fine was issued
- People who sold their car and received a fine for the new owner
- Rental car or company car drivers who received a fine

## Free Tools

- /appeal - Start a free fine assessment and generate appeal letters
- /tools/ticket-scanner - Scan and analyse your parking ticket with AI
- /tools/deadline-calculator - Calculate your appeal deadlines
- /tools/pofa-checker - Check if a private fine complies with POFA 2012
- /tools/contravention-codes - Look up contravention codes and what they mean

- /grounds/unclear-signage - Appeal based on unclear or inadequate signage

- /grounds/anpr-error - Appeal ANPR camera errors and misreads

- /grounds/loading-unloading - Appeal when loading or unloading

- /grounds/first-time-offender - Appeal as a first-time offender

## Legal Framework

FineCheck's appeal letters reference UK parking legislation including:
- Protection of Freedoms Act 2012 (POFA), Schedule 4
- Consumer Rights Act 2015
- Unfair Terms in Consumer Contracts Regulations 1999
- The Private Parking Code of Practice
- ParkingEye v Beavis [2015] UKSC 67
- Traffic Management Act 2004
- Civil Enforcement of Road Traffic Contraventions (Approved Devices, Charging and Adjudication) (England) Regulations

## Website

https://www.appealafine.co.uk
